text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members merge their code changes into a shared repository frequently, often multiple times a day. Each merge triggers an automated build, which compiles the code and runs tests to ensure everything works correctly. These CI pipelines help catch issues early, before they grow into larger problems. When the automated build or tests fail, developers are notified immediately, allowing them to fix bugs quickly. This approach saves time and improves software quality, making collaboration smoother for the whole team.
